meta.title: Nomad Letters Research Site
notes: Phase 1 derives structure and synthesis from the letters themselves.
Historical context fields are scaffolded but intentionally light until a source-linked enrichment pass is added.
Low-confidence company entities are flagged for review instead of guessed into tickers.
meta.note: Derived only from the local Nomad letters dataset; no external market data used.

answer_cards.question: What is the core philosophy in the letters?
answer_cards.answer: Consumer Value Proposition appears in 24/24 letters, Management Alignment in 23/24, and Patience & Long-Term Horizon in 20/24.

answer_cards.question: Which companies carry the recurring examples?
answer_cards.answer: Costco, Amazon, Stagecoach, Berkshire Hathaway, and Matichon are the top five recurring company examples in the extracted company mentions.
answer_cards.evidence: Counts from each letter's extracted company list.

answer_cards.question: Is this mostly a stock-picking archive or a mental-model archive?
answer_cards.answer: It is both, but the extracted structure says the mental-model layer is the durable one: the same themes recur across many different companies and years.
answer_cards.evidence: Theme recurrence is broader than any single company recurrence.

answer_cards.question: Which letters deserve a second research pass first?
answer_cards.answer: The richest starting points are the letters with the highest combined count of claims, artifacts, and company examples.
answer_cards.evidence: Ranked from claims + artifacts + company mentions per letter.

answer_cards.question: What can this dataset not answer by itself?
answer_cards.answer: It can identify what Nomad claimed and emphasized, but it cannot prove whether the claims were right without outside market, fundamentals, and macro data.
answer_cards.evidence: The local dataset contains extracted text structure, not outcome data.

research_rich_letters.title: Year End Report for December 31st, 2002
research_rich_letters.summary: This year end report centers on Consumer Value Proposition, Management Alignment, and Patience & Long-Term Horizon. It frames alignment over activity as a practical edge for partners willing to hold through noise. Named businesses in focus include Stagecoach, Georgica Plc, and Georgica. A representative thesis line is: Nor do we derive much pleasure from pointing out that the alternatives presented by the leading stock ma rket indices have fared much worse, but as we stated in the interim letter published in July, “in weak or flat…

research_rich_letters.title: Annual Letter for December 31st, 2003
research_rich_letters.summary: This annual letter centers on Consumer Value Proposition, Capital Allocation, and Management Alignment. It frames alignment over activity as a practical edge for partners willing to hold through noise. Named businesses in focus include Weetabix, Stagecoach, and Stagecoach Plc. A representative thesis line is: It may also be worth reiterating that the results have been achieved without leverage, shorting, contracts for difference, options, synthetic structures (LYONs, PRIDEs, LEAPs, MASTs - we are not making these names up)…

research_rich_letters.title: Interim Report for June 30th, 2003
research_rich_letters.summary: This interim report centers on Consumer Value Proposition, Management Alignment, and Capital Allocation. It frames alignment over activity as a practical edge for partners willing to hold through noise. Named businesses in focus include Weetabix, Weetabix Limited, and Erie Indemnity Company. A representative thesis line is: You may even want to discount the results above for the Weetabix gain (but don't ask for a rebate on fees!).

research_rich_letters.title: Interim Letter for June 30th, 2008
research_rich_letters.summary: This interim letter centers on Consumer Value Proposition, Management Alignment, and Portfolio Construction. It frames alignment over activity as a practical edge for partners willing to hold through noise. Named businesses in focus include Ryanair, Amazon, and Costco. A representative thesis line is: In our opinion it is the upper table that is most useful in assessing long - term inv estment performance.

research_rich_letters.title: Interim Letter for June 30th, 2004
research_rich_letters.summary: This interim letter centers on Consumer Value Proposition, Portfolio Construction, and Management Alignment. It frames alignment over activity as a practical edge for partners willing to hold through noise. Named businesses in focus include Marathon, Allan Gray Limited, and Browne Company. A representative thesis line is: We can promise you that any resemblance Nomad may have to the closet index trackers is entirely coincidental.

external_questions.question: Did the recurring companies outperform after they first appeared?
external_questions.needs: Market returns and benchmark data by first-mention date.
external_questions.factiq_fit: Use market data and fundamentals for tickers such as COST and AMZN.

external_questions.question: Were the letters early to consumer value and scale-economies ideas?
external_questions.needs: Company fundamentals, category prices, margins, and earnings-call language.
external_questions.factiq_fit: Use fundamentals, CPI/category data, and earnings-call search.

external_questions.question: Did patience matter more in low-rate or high-rate regimes?
external_questions.needs: Rates, inflation, market drawdowns, and recovery windows.
external_questions.factiq_fit: Use official macro data plus market series.

external_questions.question: Which claims are falsifiable versus purely interpretive?
external_questions.needs: A claim ledger linked to market, accounting, and operating outcomes.
external_questions.factiq_fit: Use FactIQ for the data-linked claims; keep interpretive claims separate.
